text: What is an inconsistent linear system?

A system with infinitely many solutions

Adding a multiple of one row to another row

text: What is the purpose of multiplying a row by a non-zero constant in elementary row operations?
options: To scale the equation represented by that row

text: Consider the augmented matrix of a linear system. What does a row of all zeros except for a non-zero entry in the last column indicate?

text: What is the leading entry of a row in a matrix?
options: The last non-zero entry in the row
The leftmost non-zero entry in the row
Any non-zero entry in the row
The largest entry in the row

text: In an echelon form matrix, where are all non-zero rows located?
options: Below any rows of all zeros
Above any rows of all zeros
In the middle of the matrix

text: A matrix is in echelon form if the leading entry of each non-zero row is to the right of the leading entry of which row?
options: Any other row in the matrix

text: In an echelon form matrix, what must be true about all entries in a column below a leading entry?
options: They can be any real number

text: Which of the following is an additional property for a matrix to be in reduced echelon form, compared to echelon form?
options: All leading entries must be even numbers
All leading entries must be 1, and are the only non-zero entries in their columns

text: Is the reduced echelon form of a matrix unique?
options: No, it depends on the elementary row operations used
Yes, every matrix has a unique reduced echelon form
Only for matrices with all non-zero entries

text: What is a pivot position in a matrix?
options: A position that contains a leading 1 in the reduced echelon form
The last entry in the last row
The first entry in the first row

text: What is a pivot column?
options: A column that contains a leading entry in the original matrix
A column that contains a pivot position
The last column of the matrix

Find the transpose of the matrix
